author: 0xpatches
content: well, it makes sense given some changes that aren't apparent between the EIP and now- it's a voting threshold, not a deadline- just confusing because it used to be a portion of a slot

author: barnabasbusa
content: there is still discussion whether we want the PTC deadline to be variable

author: barnabasbusa
content: so the timings are not set in stone yet

author: barnabasbusa
content: PTC deadline is currently at 9s I think

author: 0xpatches
content: sure- the deadline for PTC contributions- but does a client consider the payload to be timely when it arrives close to the PTC deadline?

author: barnabasbusa
content: well it will consider timely if there are more than 256 ptc votes arrive by the deadline

author: 0xpatches
content:
i believe the old logic was that the PTC contributions due at 9s declared whether the payload was seen at 6s, right?
and now we're saying a payload is timely if it is seen at all before the ptc deadline?
